title: Dachigam National Park
tagline: Dachigam National Park — wildlife reserve of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Dachigam National Park is a national park located 22 kilometres (14 mi) from Srinagar city in Srinagar district in Kashmir on the east side of Dal Lake.
overview.description: Dachigam National Park is a national park located 22 kilometres (14 mi) from Srinagar city in Srinagar district in Kashmir on the east side of Dal Lake. It covers an area of about 141 square kilometres (54 sq mi). The name of the park means "ten villages", in memory of the ten villages that were relocated for the park's formation. These ten villages were living in this region before World War I at the beginning of the 20th century.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mount Mahadev
topPlaces.description: Mahadev Peak is a mountain peak in the vicinity of New Theed in Srinagar District of Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir. It is part of the Zabarwan Range.

topPlaces.name: Zabarwan Range
topPlaces.description: The Zabarwan Range is a short (20 mi (32 km) long) sub-mountain range branching from the Great Himalayan Range in the central part of the Kashmir Valley in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir in India. Mount Mahadev, overlooking the city of Srinagar, is the range's highest peak with an elevation of 3,966 m (13,012 ft).

topPlaces.name: Marsar Lake
topPlaces.description: The Marsar Lake is an oligotrophic alpine lake located in Srinagar district in Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is located within Dachigam National Park, and is close to the Aru valley.

items.note: Mahadev Peak is a mountain peak in the vicinity of New Theed in Srinagar District of Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir. It is part of the Zabarwan Range.

The Zabarwan Range is a short (20 mi (32 km) long) sub-mountain range branching from the Great Himalayan Range in the central part of the Kashmir Valley in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir in India. Mount Mahadev, overlooking the city of Srinagar, is the range's highest peak with an elevation of 3,966 m (13,012 ft).
The Marsar Lake is an oligotrophic alpine lake located in Srinagar district in Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is located within Dachigam National Park, and is close to the Aru valley.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dachigam National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dachigam National Park is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dachigam National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (128 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dachigam National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dachigam National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dachigam National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Dachigam National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dachigam National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mount Mahadev, Zabarwan Range, Marsar Lake.
